message:
fix(swarm,policy): enforce agent ownership on work-item completion; harden command-candidate detection order

Two correctness fixes plus one minor cleanup:

1. swarm_work_queue.rs — Enforce assigned-agent ownership in finish_item():
   Previously, any agent could mark any in-progress item as completed or
   failed, even if a different agent owned the assignment. This is a race
   condition in multi-agent swarms where Agent B could accidentally
   complete Agent A's work item. The fix verifies
   assignment.agent_slot == *agent before allowing completion/failure,
   returning WorkQueueError::InvalidState on mismatch. Remaining diff is
   rustfmt reformatting (long lines broken into multi-line struct literals).

2. policy.rs — Fix is_command_candidate() check ordering:
   Moved shell metacharacter detection (&&, ||, |, >, ;, $(...), backtick)
   to BEFORE variable-assignment stripping. Previously, "FOO=$(rm -rf /)"
   would first strip "FOO=" via VAR_ASSIGN regex, potentially
   misclassifying the remainder. Now returns true immediately on subshell
   syntax. Also simplified has_trauma_bypass_prefix() to catch injection
   patterns (command substitution in assignments) rather than checking
   specific env var names.

3. blob-leases/simple_tempdir.rs — Remove unnecessary create_dir_all()
   call in path_for_content(). Content files are stored directly under
   self.root.path() (flat filenames), so the root directory already exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
